name: You're Somebody's Kink - Book Discussion
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for a thought-provoking discussion on You're Somebody's Kink, a bold and compassionate exploration of sexuality, intimacy, and the ways we define “normal” desire. Through candid interviews and deeply personal insights, the book challenges conventional ideas of sex and invites readers to rethink shame, pleasure, and connection beyond societal expectations.
Author Tanisha Rao will be in conversation with writer and community curator Rohini Malur on 10th April, from 5 PM to 6 PM. Together, they will explore the making of the book, the nuances of kink and identity, and the importance of inclusive, trauma-informed conversations around sex and pleasure.
Drawing from Tanisha’s work in sex education and Rohini’s engagement with queer literary spaces, this conversation promises to be an engaging and insightful dialogue on queerness, intimacy, and the many ways we experience and understand desire.
Join us for an open, honest, and enriching evening of conversation.

name: Qu for Quiz - Quiz
description:
The Qu for Quiz is based on a grid format with the grids formed by topics on one axis and level of difficulty (and hence the points) on the other. The topics could be varied like History, Sports, Music, Literature, Movies and more. A team of two members would get to choose a topic and the level of difficulty and the question would be revealed. The rest of the teams can go for the buzzer if they know the answer. Depending on the number of teams registering, the size of the grid would be decided. 
SAMPLE Qu: 2. The name is inspired by the traditional malt whisky distillation process in which malted barley was mixed by hand with shovels. This labor-intensive process often caused a shoulder injury to workers, causing their arms to hang low. Which brand is named thus? 
Ganesh Balakrishnan is an entrepreneur, marketer and creator of terrible dad jokes. His claim to fame is attempting to sell shoes during the pandemic, culminating in a viral Shark Tank "Down But Not Out" appearance. He is a collector of useless facts since childhood, and has dabbled in trivia quiz circuits since college. ganesh.flatheads@gmail.com

Karthik Srinivasan is a pseudo-retired software leader by choice, specializing in innovative ideas to spend time that include organizing cricket leagues at the scale of IPL but for free, staying up to date on latest debates in virtual social groups helmed by family and friends and more on similar lines. emailkarth@gmail.com
performer: by Karthik Srinivasan and Ganesh Balakrishnan

name: The Paper Sanctuary April 11-12 2026, 11 AM onwards - A  Festival of Stationery
description:
Inspired by the quiet, hidden bookshops of Japanese fiction, The Paper Sanctuary transforms Atta Galatta into a retreat for the tactile and the thoughtful.
As a hundred paper lanterns flicker to life, the modern world retreats. Floor-to-ceiling bookshelves are cast in amber shadows, and the bookstore’s red-brick walls become a backdrop for a different kind of commerce—one that values the texture of a page over the speed of a screen.

At The Paper Sanctuary, we value the small joys over productivity. Come browse the stalls, find your new favorite notebook, and enjoy a weekend dedicated to the art of writing and more.

Age: All

name: ಪತ್ತೇದಾರ ಪ್ರಣವ ಕೇಸ್ 2! - Book Launch
description:
Join us for an exciting book launch of ಪತ್ತೇದಾರ ಪ್ರಣವ ಕೇಸ್ 2! 🕵️‍♂️📖
Step into the world of mystery with a fun read-aloud session by the author, Anupama K Benachinamardi.
Kids will enjoy mysterious games, interactive moments, and learn simple Kannada in a fun and easy way.
✨ A perfect blend of stories, play, and learning for young readers!
This event is FREE for all!

name: Atta Galatta presents “AG Book Club” - Monthly Book Club
description:
What have you been reading? Come, discuss your latest read. Each member gets 5-7 minutes to talk about the book, theme, plot, with 2 minutes to answer any questions others might have. This month’s special focus will be Sports/Music- come tell us what you've been reading.
April’s special focus will be on Sports and Music

name: Mahabharata: What They Don’t Tell You - A storytelling deep-dive
description:
Imagine ancient India’s most chaotic family group chat just went public. We are talking about a world where every hero is a walking red flag, every villain has receipts, and the drama is so intense that it burns an entire civilization to the ground. 
Does this mean that we are taking liberties with this ancient story? Absolutely not. This is a deep - dive  oral telling of the Mahabharata in all its gritty, timeless glory. We are honoring our roots while calling out the sheer human messiness of scandals, betrayals and forbidden desires that makes these characters immortal. 
So irrespective of your first tryst with the Mahabharata – The 80’s TV show, Grandparents’ bedtime tales, or viral reels – this performance is for you.

name: Coffee and Conversations - Group Meetup
description:
What about some Filter Coffee and Unfiltered conversations on a fine Sunday morning?
Listeners Circle is an initiative to facilitate listening without judgment, hence giving a safe space for anyone to speak their heart out without the fear of judgment.
We at Listeners Circle are committed to building a community where there are no unsaid words, held back emotions and buried feelings because of the fear of being judged.
After 50+ Editions of Tell your Story, Listeners Circle relaunching a lighter format - Coffee and Conversations!
Coffee lightens us up. Coffee gives us clarity. Coffee refreshes up. Sip a coffee and speak your heart out without fear of judgment and listen without judging! Coffee is on us and the onus of conversation without judgment is on you.
Every word matters. Every emotion matters. Every feeling matters.
Think about how it would feel to take an hour out of your fast-paced life and share your experiences and thoughts to people who listen to you without judging over a cup of Coffee? Also, listen to others without judging.
Listeners` circle Coffee and Conversations -This is an event to make that happen.
Be a part of the Listener`s circle Coffee & Conversations - Edition 5.
Speak your mind without being judged. Speak your mind without any boundaries. No photography and videography without consent. Your stories stay within the four walls of the room and the hearts.
Would you like to just listen? We have a place for you too.
Book your passes in advance to avoid the last-minute sell outs.
Listeners Circle is an initiative to facilitate listening without judgment, hence giving a safe space for anyone to speak their heart out without the fear of judgment. We have done 50+ sessions over the last year, enabling people to voice their stories. Coffee & Conversations is our new format based on popular demand.

name: The Paper Sanctuary April 11-12 2026, 11 AM onwards - A  Festival of Stationery
description:
Inspired by the quiet, hidden bookshops of Japanese fiction, The Paper Sanctuary transforms Atta Galatta into a retreat for the tactile and the thoughtful.
As a hundred paper lanterns flicker to life, the modern world retreats. Floor-to-ceiling bookshelves are cast in amber shadows, and the bookstore’s red-brick walls become a backdrop for a different kind of commerce—one that values the texture of a page over the speed of a screen.

At The Paper Sanctuary, we value the small joys over productivity. Come browse the stalls, find your new favorite notebook, and enjoy a weekend dedicated to the art of writing and more.

Age: All

name: Tiny Picasso Fiasco - Doodling Workshop
description:
A playful, hands-on doodling session designed for young creators (5+) to explore art freely and joyfully.
At Tiny Picasso Fiasco, children are encouraged to let go of “perfect drawing” and simply enjoy the process of creating. With crayons, sketch pens, and a whole lot of imagination, they’ll experiment with lines, shapes, patterns, and colours, where drawing outside the lines is not just allowed, but celebrated.
The session focuses on creative expression, confidence-building, and sensory play, while our facilitators gently guide and engage children through fun prompts and activities. Expect happy hands, curious minds, and lots of proud little artworks to take home.
Come as you are. Stay as you feel. Leave with something that feels like yours.

Age: 5+

name: History Unpacked: The Why, When and What of Medieval India - Book Launch
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta as Saisudha Acharya and Rohit launch their new book, History Unpacked: The Why, When and What of Medieval India, a lively and engaging dive into a thousand years of the subcontinent’s past. Filled with daring rulers, adventurous journeys, shifting empires, and unexpected global encounters—from the Cholas and Delhi Sultans to Vijayanagara and the arrival of the Portuguese—the book brings history to life through timelines, comics, quizzes, and playful storytelling.
The evening will also explore how history is told, whose voices are remembered, and why these stories continue to shape our present.
Complementing the conversation, experience the enduring musical tradition of the Bhakti movement with Namma Kutcheri—a Bengaluru-based Carnatic ensemble—creating a unique blend of history, storytelling, and music.
Age: 10+
performer: by Saisudha Acharya and Rohit Bhasi

name: Zine & Tonic - Zine Making Workshop
description:
A little something for the soul: best served with colours and paper.
Zine & Tonic is a creative unwind for tired adults who need a break from the constant doing, fixing, and holding it all together. Think of it as your evening “tonic”, where instead of talking things out, you get to make your way through them.
In this workshop, you’ll create your own zine, a small, personal booklet that can hold your thoughts, feelings, questions, and stories in whatever form feels right. Words, doodles, scraps, half-finished ideas, it all belongs here.
We’ll gently explore themes like the lives we live vs. the lives we imagine, and the small, quiet parts of ourselves that often get overlooked.
No art experience needed. All materials are provided. Our facilitators will guide you with thoughtful prompts and just enough structure to help you begin; the rest is yours to shape.
Come as you are. Stay as you feel. Leave with something that feels like yours.
Age: 18+

name: The Heart’s Colour Palette and The Inside Sales Evolution - Dual Book Launch ✨
description:
Join us for an inspiring evening of stories, ideas, and meaningful conversations as we celebrate the launch of two remarkable books at Atta Galatta, Indiranagar.
This special event brings together two unique voices—Raadika Viswanathan, author of The Heart’s Colour Palette, and Bala Ji, the Coach, author of The Inside Sales Evolution. While one explores the depth of human emotions through art, reflection, and storytelling, the other offers powerful insights into the world of sales, growth, and transformation.
Set in the warm and welcoming space of Atta Galatta, the evening will feature engaging author interactions, reflections on the writing journey, and an opportunity to connect with ideas that inspire both the heart and the mind.
Whether you are a reader, a writer, or someone seeking a moment of inspiration, this event promises to leave you with new perspectives and meaningful takeaways.
📅 Date: 15 April 2026, Wednesday ⏰ Time: 6:00 PM – 7:00 PM 📍 Venue: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
Come, be a part of an evening that celebrates creativity, knowledge, and heartfelt conversations.
performer: by Raadika and Bala Ji the Coach

name: Folded Away Softly and Soul Stitched - Book launch
description:
Poetry is a form of art that uses words as a paintbrush to paint memories on the canvas of life. April the 16th marks several historical events like the launch of India's first passenger train and National Voice Day. Poetry is one beautiful form of expressing in a silent yet emotional voice. We invite you to be present for the launch of two collections of poems written by two beautiful poets - Mangal D. Karnad and Keerthi Sudhakar. This invitation is from Ayaz Zanzeria, Chairman of the Rotary Book Readers Fellowship. Please be present at Atta Galatta on the 16th of April 2026 at 5.00 pm India time.
Age: 18+
performer: by Mangal D. Karnad and Keerthi Sudhaka

name: Reboot With Art - Journaling workshop
description:
An art journaling session where we let go of control and just play.
No pens. No pencils. No erasing. Just colour, instinct, and a little bit of chaos.
We’re rebooting our minds for the weekend—with whimsy, mess, and freedom.
Colour outside the lines. Actually, we insist on it.
All materials included.
And with prices rising everywhere…
Do you really want to miss something that lets you feel this free?
Age: 18+

name: Muqadamma – The Trial - A Poetic Musical courtroom drama of Three Witnesses
description:
Muqadamma unfolds through three witnesses —
one who never spoke,
one who chose silence,
and one who was silenced… until even the ruins began to speak.
Created and performed by Ruha Latif — a practicing civil litigation advocate, a woman’s rights activist and a speaker on gender discrimination and Shariah rights of Women, the author of “Rooh Dekhi Hai Kabhi (Part 1 & 2)”, Ballads from a Mother’s Heart & Conversations, and a performing artist who bridges courtroom and poetry.
Accompanied by live violin and mandolin by Utsav Biswas , he has trained in both Indian and western classical music. He loves to explore how emotions and music intertwine. 
This gathering is more like witnessing a courtroom trial.

Age: all

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Food is more than sustenance; it is a map of our memories and a bridge to different cultures. This  month, we continue our dialogue on the narratives that connect what we read to what we eat. What does it mean to understand a civilization through its appetite? 
This April, we explore the intricate, technique-driven world of Chinese gastronomy through Fuchsia Dunlop’s masterful narrative. We are moving beyond the surface of a meal to examine how history, ritual, and culture  are served at a single table.
 
Book of the month: Invitation to a Banquet: The Story of Chinese Food by Fuchsia Dunlop
When: Saturday, 18th April, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots (link in bio)
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who to love to read and eat!

name: Rainbow Journal - Meet-up and  Workshop
description:
Rainbow Journaling is a creative, reflective workshop that invites you to explore your thoughts, stories, and emotions through colour and words.
Using guided prompts, colours, and simple journaling exercises, this session creates a relaxed space to slow down, reflect, and put your thoughts onto the page. Think of it as a pause from the noise of everyday life — a chance to sit with your ideas, express yourself freely, and see what emerges.
This is not about perfect writing or polished storytelling. It’s about curiosity, creativity, and letting the page hold whatever you feel like expressing.
✨ Guided prompts to spark reflection
✨ Creative journaling using colour and words
✨ A calm, welcoming space to write and think
✨ A chance to explore the many shades of your story
Come as you are... all you need is an open mind and a willingness to write.
Age: 18 and above

name: Pop-up Choir - A Musical Session
description:
525,600 minutes… how do you measure a year? ❤️

This April 18th, we step into the world of musicals for the very first time with Seasons of Love from Rent.

Leading us this session is the beautiful and kind, Choir Director Amy Joseph (who is no stranger to leading large choirs).

Come sing, feel, and celebrate the moments that matter

Let’s measure this one in love. Save your spot, registrations are open, link is below.

Age: 15+

name: Indie Authors Social - An Intimate Creator’s Meet Up
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta, right by Indiranagar Metro, for a unique Indie Authors Social! This event is inspired by the real struggles indie authors face. You’ll connect with fellow writers, gain clarity on your 1- to 3-year goals, and share support to collaborate—because no one should have to succeed alone. Building on the success of two Creator Socials, this is our next step in helping authors make their dreams a reality. Let’s rise together!
While there have been author conferences and book fairs, this event stands apart. Unlike large, general gatherings, this Indie Authors Social focuses on intimate, goal-driven collaboration. Drawing on real indie author struggles, it’s not just about networking—it’s about finding partners to help each other succeed. While similar themes exist globally, this rare event offers a local, personal touch—ensuring indie authors don’t walk their journey
Ready to grow your author journey? Click the link in our bio now to secure your spot—spaces are limited, and this year’s event won’t happen again until next year.
 
Detailed flow of the event:
Connection (First 30 mins):
We’ll begin with icebreakers to help authors get to know each other, so they can identify potential collaborators right from the start.
Clarity (Next 30 mins):
Authors will clarify their goals—whether for 1, 2, or 3 years—and reflect on their target audience, their book, and key challenges.
Sharing & Exchange (30 mins):
Attendees will discuss what they need to reach their goals and what they can offer others—building mutual support.
Goal Framework (30 mins):
Authors will outline a simple plan—key steps they need to take and who they might seek help from.
Mastermind Groups (30 mins):
We’ll form groups of four indie authors and one aspiring author, creating supportive circles.
Reflection (Final 30 mins):
We’ll wrap up by reflecting on the event, and each author will commit to what they will achieve in the coming year.
Age: 18+

name: Chocolate & Stories - A Guided Chocolate Tasting and Storytelling Experience
description:
Step into a slow, sensory afternoon of chocolate with AMAY.  
This intimate experience invites you to explore handcrafted chocolates through flavour, texture, and storytelling. As you sample a curated selection of artisanal creations, you’ll discover the nuances of cocoa, the craft behind each piece, and the stories that bring them to life.  
Hosted by Sreya, Founder of AMAY Dessert Studio, this session is designed to be warm, engaging, and experiential — perfect for chocolate lovers and curious minds.  

Age: 16+

name: The Bangalore Finance Club (BFC) Presents: Women and the World of Finance - Financial Literacy Workshop
description:
The Bangalore Finance Club (BFC) is a premier community hub dedicated to fostering financial literacy, professional growth, and networking within the vibrant financial landscape of Bangalore. Our mission is to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and industry practice, providing a platform where enthusiasts, professionals, and aspiring leaders can exchange ideas, master market trends, and build lasting connections.
Event Spotlight: Women and the World of Finance
For our inaugural event, we are proud to host a session that explores the multifaceted relationship between Women and the World of Finance. This event is designed to be both a masterclass in personal wealth management and a strategic roadmap for professional success, focusing on two critical pillars:
 
1. The Investment Journey: Tailored for Women
Wealth creation is not one-size-fits-all. We will discuss why a distinct approach to investing is essential for women, considering factors like longer life expectancy and career fluidity.
• Navigating Options: Deep dives into Mutual Funds, SIPs, Gold, and Equity.
• The Importance of "Why": Understanding the long-term impact of financial independence and compounding.
• Practical Ownership: How to start, manage, and scale your personal investments with confidence.
 
2. Opportunities and Careers in Finance
The financial sector offers vast potential, yet the path to the top often requires a unique set of navigation tools.
• The Landscape: Exploring roles across Asset Management, Banking, and Fintech.
• Navigating Challenges: Real talk on overcoming hurdles, managing career transitions, and breaking through professional ceilings.
• Career Growth: Strategies for networking, mentorship, and building a successful, high-impact career.
 
Meet Our Guest Speaker: Chithra AS
We are honoured to welcome Chithra AS, a true veteran of the Indian financial sector, to lead our first-ever event.
With over 25 years of experience, Chithra currently serves as the Executive Vice President and South Head at Kotak Asset Management Company. Her career spans nearly two decades at the Kotak Group following five years at Citibank, giving her an unparalleled perspective on the evolution of Indian finance. As a senior leader at one of India’s premier AMC firms, she brings the perfect blend of institutional expertise and personal wisdom to this discussion.
Join us for an afternoon of insightful dialogue, networking, and empowerment.
Age: 18-80 yrs

name: Akshaya Tritiya Handwriting Workshop - Improve Your Penmanship
description:
This Workshop is for all age groups and starts from beginner’s level. Akshaya Tritiya is an auspicious day. On this day the participants shall be guided to write ‘Akshaya Tritiya Affirmations’ in Calligraphic style. The worksheets are designed to start from ZERO level. Then related artworks and motifs shall be practiced to add creativity to your thoughts. These artworks adopt a step-by-step approach. Now all is set to explore better ways to create a wonderful frame-worthy poster. All materials provided. Your artwork is your asset.
About Facilitator:
Chandran A, is Certified Microsoft Innovative Educator (2012), AIMS-WeSchool Innovation Awardee (2018). CSR Practitioner through various volunteering initiatives and has completed more than two decades of teaching assignments.
Ages: 10-80 yrs (all ages welcome)

name: A Summer of Creativity - 5 day Summer Art Camp
description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..
Let’s swap that for creativity 💫
Join Summer Art Camp for a fun-filled 5 days where kids explore different art mediums, express themselves, and take home their own creations 🎨
✨ Canvas painting
✨ Fluid art 
✨ Upcycled coconut shell art
✨ Hand-building with clay
✨Collage making ( Self-portrait )
 
⏰ 11 AM – 1 PM
📍 Atta Galatta
💰 ₹5000 (5 days) | ₹1200 (single day)
🎨 All materials included
Limited spots only!
Register now via the link in AG’s bio 💛
Age: 6-13 yrs

name: A Summer of Creativity - 5 day Summer Art Camp
description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..
Let’s swap that for creativity 💫
Join Summer Art Camp for a fun-filled 5 days where kids explore different art mediums, express themselves, and take home their own creations 🎨

🎨 All materials included
Limited spots only!
Register now via the link in AG’s bio 💛
Age: 6-13 yrs

name: Emergency Art Break - Art workshop
description:
We have an emergency.
And it’s your mind refusing to calm the f down.
So… pause.
Grab a journal, a sketchbook—or honestly, just yourself—and come sit with us.
 
This is your first aid kit:
colors, stickers, playful prompts, and a little bit of unexpected chaos.
No pressure to be good.
No pressure to make sense.
Just somewhere to land.
Consider this your official call for help.
And also your softest way out of the spiral.
 
Age: 18+

name: The Adventures of Rifa - Book Reading and Signing
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the book launch of The Adventures of Rifa by Samina Mahmood, a charming children’s book that brings alive the joys, mischief and wonder of growing up in a hill station in the 1950s.
Through the eyes of Rifa—curious, courageous and delightfully mischievous—the book follows a series of small but memorable adventures shared with her brother, cousins and friends. Set against the backdrop of sun-dappled days and rainy afternoons, these stories capture the innocence of childhood while gently reflecting on the lessons, values and friendships that shape us as we grow.
Join us for a warm and engaging session with the author as she introduces young readers to Rifa’s world and shares the memories and inspirations behind the book.

name: Keeping A Sketchbook Diary - an introduction to gouache techniques
description:
Have you ever wanted to capture a moment — not just with a photo, but with your hands? This workshop is an invitation to slow down, look closely, and make something that is entirely yours.
In two hours, you'll move through three acts: first, a set of guided observation exercises designed to train your eye — noticing colour, negative space, and the shapes hiding in plain sight. Then, a reflection practice using poetry, short passages, and journal prompts to find the words for what you've seen. Finally, integration — weaving your sketches and writing into a single page, and learning how to carry this practice home.
No experience needed. Gouache is a forgiving, vibrant medium — bright payoff, slow pace, good company.
All Materials are included.
Age: 16+

name: THE COMMUNITY ART CIRCLE - Wonders Of Team Work In Art And Story Making
description:
A fun take on art with a community team spirit.
Seated in a circle layout, the imagination comes to life with basic art skills, inspiring stories and creative expressions.
Take home a creation inspiring stories, memories and a lot of interpretations!
Art. Story. Repeat!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.

name: A Summer of Creativity - 5 day Summer Art Camp
description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..

🎨 All materials included
Limited spots only!
Register now via the link in AG’s bio 💛
Age: 6-13 yrs

name: Mantras of the Moon - Book Launch & Poetry Reading
description:
Atta Galatta invites you to an evening of poetry, conversation, and reflection with poet Srijani Mitra, celebrating the launch of her new poetry collection Mantras of the Moon.
Published by Red Rook Press, Mantras of the Moon is a lyrical exploration of identity, cultural inheritance, memory, and selfhood. Through meditative and evocative poems, the collection navigates questions of belonging, Indianness, spirituality, and the inner landscapes that shape who we become.
In this special launch event, Srijani Mitra will read from the collection, discuss the ideas and inspirations behind the book, and engage in conversation with the audience about poetry, culture, and the creative process.
About the Author:
Srijani Mitra is a poet and writer whose work explores the intersections of culture, selfhood, and language. She has presented her work in academic and literary spaces including a talk organized by Indian Institute of Technology Dharwad. Mantras of the Moon marks an important addition to contemporary poetry engaging with cultural identity.
Join us for an intimate literary evening celebrating poetry and the power of voice.
Age: 16 and above

name: THE ROAR AND THE SQUEAK! - Puppet Show, Art Session And Some Fun-Time
description:
The mighty lion and the tiny mouse return in a charming puppet show filled with sing-along songs and storytelling through handmade puppets. Children will then be guided through an art activity to create an animated lion – the imaginative storytelling version of the character on paper!
A perfect blend of art, imagination, and storytelling: come and create your own jungle tale!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.
Art. Story. Repeat!

name: The Darker Moon - Book Reading and Signing
description:
Inviting everyone to a book reading by Syamanthaka, where she will read a few pages from her debut fiction novel – The Darker New Moon.
An IT professional, exploring her creative side, she would answer a few audience questions on how she self-published the book.
She would also be super happy to sign your copy of The Darker New Moon.
---
This event is open to all ages, although the genre itself is more suitable for “grown ups” who love reading a good mystery.

name: A Love Story of 12.5 Lacs - Solo Storytelling Act
description:
A software engineer chasing his acting dreams falls in love with a young aspiring actress. As they navigate auditions and struggles, their bond deepens—until one unexpected incident changes everything.
Without offering a real reason, she asks to break up, leaving him confused, heartbroken, and searching for answers.
Years later, during the COVID pandemic, a single message brings the past rushing back—with just one word: “Sorry.”
A poignant tale of love, silence, and unanswered questions that never truly fade.
Written and performed by Vaibhav Mishra, this solo act blends reality with fiction—promising moments that will feel deeply personal and relatable to everyone.
Age: 16+

name: Hired. Fired. Tired. - Book Launch
description:
Come join us for an afternoon of fun, banter, empowerment, and honest conversations on womanhood, workplace nightmares, women’s leadership, cognitive biases-and much more.
On May 24, join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Hired. Fired. Tired. by debut author Tushara Reddy- a candid and thought-provoking book exploring the realities of the modern tech workplace. Framed through the perspective of a first-generation millennial professional, the book unpacks the often-unspoken politics of the industry-where ambition, identity, and survival frequently collide.
Told from a woman’s point of view, Hired. Fired. Tired. speaks to those navigating toxic work cultures, imposter syndrome, layoffs, and the quiet pressures of being a newcomer. At once deeply personal and broadly relatable, the book gives voice to the silent struggles of everyday work life while imagining bold possibilities for a more humane and equitable tech future. Tushara also invites readers to be part of her personal journey of writing toward a more utopian tech world-one where empathy, fairness, and opportunity shape the future of work.
As a bonus, the evening will also feature a panel discussion with global tech leaders, exploring real stories, challenges, and ideas for building healthier workplaces in the tech industry.
Tushara Reddy is a mid-senior product manager with over nine years of experience across India, the UK, and the USA. An alumna of Queen Mary University of London, she currently lives in Bengaluru, where she balances work, workouts, and writing in her spare time.

Age: 18 and above
